items.title: Nigerian lawmakers pass bill to compel NGOs to disclose foreign donors for second reading
items.description: The lawmakers said billions of naira in foreign aid are channeled to NGOs operating in Nigeria without adequate scrutiny of the sources of the funds or the purposes for which they are donated, adding that it poses serious national security risks and undermines public accountability. The post Nigerian lawmakers pass bill to compel NGOs to disclose foreign donors for second reading appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Insect repellent release leaves 11 staff sick at major SA hospital
items.description: Eleven staff at an Adelaide hospital have become unwell and a dozen emergency beds have been closed, after the accidental release of an "outdoor insect repellent" inside the facility.

items.title: Modi vows 'stringent punishment' for exam fraud fuelling India's youth-led 'Cockroach' protests
items.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Thursday vowed to punish those involved in a leaked exam papers scandal that has sparked mass student protests. His promise came a day after thousands of protesters, led by the social media-driven Cockroach Janta Party, demonstrated in New Delhi, sparking fresh clashes with police. The snowballing protests have become one of the biggest challenges to Modi since his re-election for a third term in office in 2024.

items.title: World Cup halftime performers see jump in song streams
items.description: It was the 11-minute performance heard around the world. Madonna, Justin Bieber, BTS and Shakira each brought abridged hits to the World Cup’s first halftime show in a whirlwind performance Sunday just outside New York. And it has resulted in some noteworthy streaming bumps.

Leaders warned that cuts in international aid and limited domestic investment are threatening decades of progress against diseases including HIV, malaria and tuberculosis.

items.title: France's D-Day landing beaches could become a UNESCO World Heritage site in July
items.description: Normandy’s application to have the D-Day beaches in northern France listed as a UNESCO World Heritage site could finally be accepted at the end of July, after it's submission nearly 20 years ago. The nomination also includes Pointe du Hoc, the German artillery battery at Longues-sur-Mer, Port Mulberry, and numerous military cemeteries and memorials. The UN cultural agency is set to male a decision between July 24 and 27.

items.title: Coal mine extensions and data centres hindering NSW net zero targets
items.description: The NSW Net Zero Commission recommends tighter planning controls for the approval of coal extensions and data centres, as it predicts the state will fall short of its legislated 2030 emissions reduction target.

items.title: Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' storms the box office
items.description: Christopher Nolan’s “The Odyssey” launched with an estimated $124.5 million in US ticket sales and another $139.6 million from overseas, notching an even better debut than “Oppenheimer” and marking the filmmaker’s best opening since 2012’s “The Dark Knight Rises.”

items.title: Climate change has intensified Europe's risk of drought, scientists say
items.description: Human-caused climate change has intensified Europe's drought by making extreme heat, rather than low rainfall, the main driver of exceptionally dry conditions, an international team of scientists said on Thursday, warning that a warmer atmosphere is sharply increasing drought and wildfire risks.

items.description: The U.S. faces a complex challenge at Iran’s Pickaxe Mountain, where powerful bunker buster bombs face hardy geological formations. Success is also limited by a critical minerals crunch, and could force Trump to shift from total destruction to disabling surface infrastructure and subterranean access tunnels

items.title: Germany's growing interest in Africa: A win for both sides?

The 54-year-old coach will sign a four-year contract, until the 2030 World Cup. He will bring an end to the Didier Deschamps era, who has been in charge of Les Bleus since 2012.

items.description: Iran’s demands over Strait of Hormuz would set dangerous precedent, says Rubio; Jordan’s military said it intercepted four missiles in the Iranian attack

items.title: Tearful fights, edible offerings – a glimpse into the Diego Maradona death trial
items.description: A look at the standout moments of an often dramatic case; Defendants, who insist Maradona died of natural causes, have spent months in a courtroom alongside the footballer's distraught daughters. Leer más

items.title: Extreme heatwave grips Tunisia as wide power cuts fuel frustration
items.description: In Tunisia long power outages across the country have added to the strain of an intense heatwave, with temperatures as high as 49C, leaving many fearing for their livelihoods.

items.title: Daily Quiz | Notable events of July 22 in history
items.description: Try to test your knowledge on some very notable events of this day in history

items.title: Spain wins second World Cup after beating Argentina in final
items.description: After a 16-year wait, Spain has claimed a second men's World Cup. Ferran Torres scored the winner in a deserved 1-0 victory over Argentina, which means Spain is now men's and women's world champions.

items.title: Wreckage of Pan Am plane that shaped aviation safety found 74 years on
items.description: The deaths of 52 people on the Clipper Endeavor led to the introduction of mandatory pre-flight safety briefings.

items.title: ECOWAS reaffirms plans to launch single currency in 2027
items.description: The Economic Community of West African States, or ECOWAS, has reaffirmed plans to launch a single currency next year. But the bloc says it’s leaning towards a phased rollout.

items.title: Meet Andy Burnham, Britain's new prime minister
items.description: Andy Burnham has been sworn in as the successor to British Prime Minister Keir Starmer. Burnham, the former Manchester mayor, has become a leading figure in the Labour Party and one of the UK's most popular politicians.

items.title: 2,000 volunteers join a painstaking effort to restore massive skeleton of prehistoric predator
items.description: Mastodonsaurus lived about 240 million years ago during the Middle Triassic period. It was an underwater ambush hunter, preying mainly on fish and smaller amphibians

items.title: “The winning mentality starts here” – Awoniyi targets strong Nottingham Forest season after pre-season goal
items.description: Awoniyi, who endured a frustrating 2025/26 campaign due to injuries and inconsistent form, delivered another encouraging display as he continued his resurgence ahead of the new season The post “The winning mentality starts here” – Awoniyi targets strong Nottingham Forest season after pre-season goal appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Taiwanese woman sentenced for attempted murder of Australian exchange student
items.description: Taiwanese media has reported Yang Jinping has been sentenced for the attempted murder of Brisbane exchange student Alex Shorey in 2023.

items.title: Venezuela earthquakes death toll rises to 5,278
items.description: National Assembly President Jorge Rodriguez said on Telegram the death toll from the quakes reached at least 5,278, while the number reported injured remained at 16,740.

items.title: Philipsen wins Tour de France 17th stage as Pogacar retains overall lead
items.description: Belgium's Jasper Philipsen won the 17th stage of the 2026 Tour de France on Wednesday with a comfortable lead ahead of Mauro Schmid and Olav Kooij at the end of the Alpine stage. While Tadej Pogacar struggled throughout the stage after suffering from a stomach problem the previous night, the Slovenian cyclist remains in the lead.

items.title: Jailed opposition leader Ghannouchi faints in Tunisian prison due to heat
items.description: The incident was witnessed by the daughter of Ahmed Nejib Chebbi, another prominent political prisoner.

items.title: DRC's opposition cancel protest after president announces national dialogue
items.description: Opposition parties in the Democratic Republic of the Congo are postponing a protest after President Felix Tshisekedi announced the launch of a national dialogue.

items.title: EXCLUSIVE: Permanent Secretary blocks release of funds approved by Tinubu for agencies
items.description: The permanent secretary allegedly refused to approve the payment files because the heads of the agencies under the Federal Ministry of Information and National Orientation declined her request to appear before her. The post EXCLUSIVE: Permanent Secretary blocks release of funds approved by Tinubu for agencies appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Canada's 'powerful' dairy sector is in Trump's trade crosshairs
items.description: Canada's dairy supply management system is in the spotlight after the US president singled it out as one of three main irritants used to justify new tariffs.

items.title: Female coaches want more representation in African football
items.description: Female head coaches will be a rare sight at the Women’s African Cup of Nations. However, there are some promising signs.

items.description: A string of dramatic comebacks and extra-time victories fuels confidence that the Albiceleste will fight until the very end – and succeed. Leer más

items.description: Pete Hegseth testified during a Senate Appropriations Committee hearing at which he defended a request for $67 billion in supplemental funding for the Pentagon

items.title: US hits Iran as Houthis claim strikes on Saudi tankers
items.description: The US is carrying out its 12th night of strikes on Iran. Meanwhile, Yemen's Houthis are claiming attacks on Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea. DW has the latest.

items.title: Milei opens door to electric cars as Musk's Tesla firms up arrival plans
items.description: Argentina’s car market is undergoing a silent but fast-moving revolution. While the world's most eccentric mobility brand, Elon Musk’s Telsa, prepares for its arrival in the national market, the landscape has already been transformed by new Asian giants. Leer más

items.title: Arsenal defender Saliba sidelined for ‘extended period’ due to back injury
items.description: Centre back William Saliba won't require surgery for injury sustained at World Cup, his club says.

items.title: Who is Mikhailo Drapaty, Ukraine's new commander-in-chief?
items.description: Volodymyr Zelensky has bowed to pressure from the streets. The former head of the Ukrainian armed forces Oleksandr Syrsky has been replaced by the popular Mykhailo Drapaty. Who is he?

items.title: Messi eyes glorious farewell as Spain, Argentina clash in World Cup final
items.description: Holders Argentina are bidding to become the first team in 64 years to successfully defend the title, in what likely to be the final World Cup match of captain Lionel Messi's career. Leer más

items.title: What's next for the Albiceleste after World Cup final defeat?
items.description: Argentina fell agonisingly short of winning a second consecutive World Cup title. As the team reaches the end of an era, speculation begins over what comes next. Leer más

items.title: South Africa signs $1.5bn World Bank loan for infrastructure development
items.description: It will help the country confront challenges in electricity supply, freight transport, and water and sanitation services that have long constrained economic growth

items.title: Africa surpasses Asia with highest population facing hunger: UN
items.description: The UN's annual report on food security and nutrition found that there has been gradual improvement towards reducing hunger overall in recent years, but that progress has been uneven

items.title: India: Modi orders fast-track courts for paper leaks amid protests
items.description: India's PM Narendra Modi has announced speedy trials to punish those involved in paper leaks, without directly commenting on the ongoing student protests across the nation. DW has more.

items.title: Congo: Ebola deaths increase to over 1,000
items.description: The fatality rate of the latest Ebola virus outbreak since mid-May is far higher than in previous outbreaks. No vaccines or treatments exist for the new strain.

items.title: How Russia is luring foreign nationals to fight in Ukraine
items.description: More and more, Russia is recruiting men from the world’s poorest countries to send them to the front in the Ukraine war. Why are people joining up?

items.title: Africa overtakes Asia as world's hunger hot spot, UN report finds
items.description: Africa has overtaken Asia as the world’s hunger hotspot and could soon be home to almost 60 percent of the world’s chronically undernourished people.

items.title: Germany records rise in sexual violence against children
items.description: Germany's Criminal Police Office has reported over 17,000 cases of sexual violence against children in the past year. Organizations such as Zartbitter provide support to victims.
